Record Class JavelitChatUI

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
com.embabel.examples.ragbot.javelit.JavelitChatUI
Record Components:
chatbot - chatbot facade that routes messages into Embabel actions
properties - application settings (voice, objective, UI port, CSS path)
searchOperations - search store used for showing live RAG statistics
@Component public record JavelitChatUI(com.embabel.chat.Chatbot chatbot, RagbotProperties properties, com.embabel.agent.rag.lucene.LuceneSearchOperations searchOperations) extends Record
Provides a Javelit-based web UI for the RAG chatbot.

This record provides a browser-based chat interface as an alternative to Spring Shell. The UI keeps a per-browser-session ChatSession, sends user messages to the shared Chatbot, and renders assistant responses from an in-memory queue. Because it reuses the same Chatbot bean as shell commands, behavior remains consistent across text and browser interactions.

  • Constructor Details

    • JavelitChatUI

      public JavelitChatUI(com.embabel.chat.Chatbot chatbot, RagbotProperties properties, com.embabel.agent.rag.lucene.LuceneSearchOperations searchOperations)

      searchOperations - the value for the searchOperations record component

  • Method Details

    • start

      public String start(int port)
      Starts the web chat UI on a specific port and opens a browser window.
      Parameters:
      port - port number to bind the embedded Javelit server to
      Returns:
      Returns URL of the started UI, for example http://localhost:8888

    • start

      public String start(boolean openBrowser)
      Starts the web chat UI using the configured default port.

      This is the path used by the uichat shell command when no port is provided explicitly.

      Parameters:
      openBrowser - whether to attempt opening the default browser automatically
      Returns:
      Returns URL of the started UI

    • start

      public String start(int port, boolean openBrowser)
      Starts the web chat UI with explicit control over port and browser behavior.

      Best practice: this method enforces single-server startup by checking serverRef first, preventing accidental duplicate server instances.

      Parameters:
      port - port number to bind
      openBrowser - whether to try opening the UI URL in the desktop browser
      Returns:
      Returns URL of the running UI or existing instance message
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if server startup fails

    • start

      public String start()
      Starts the UI on the configured port and opens the browser.
      Returns:
      Returns URL of the running UI

    • openInBrowser

      private void openInBrowser(String url)
      Tries to open a URL in the system browser.

      The implementation first uses Java's Desktop API and then falls back to platform-specific commands (open, start, xdg-open).

      Parameters:
      url - url to open

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the running UI server if one exists.

    • isRunning

      public boolean isRunning()
      Indicates whether the UI server is currently running.
      Returns:
      Returns true when a server instance is active; otherwise false

    • app

      private void app()
      Entry point for each HTTP interaction handled by Javelit.

      This wrapper centralizes exception handling so the browser receives a friendly error instead of an unhandled stack trace.

    • doApp

      private void doApp()
      Renders the chat page and processes user input for the current browser session.

      Session state keys used:

      • displayHistory: ordered messages rendered in the UI.
      • chatSession: Embabel chat session used to send/receive messages.
      • responseQueue: queue receiving assistant messages from output channel.
      The page also surfaces configured objective/persona values and current RAG store stats so users can see response context while chatting.

    • resolveCssPath

      private String resolveCssPath()
      Resolves configured CSS resource to a filesystem path for Javelit's headers file.

      If the configured resource cannot be accessed as a regular file (common for classpath resources inside jars), this method copies it to a temporary file.

      Returns:
      Returns absolute path to a CSS file, or null if resolution fails

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
